Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event

99 attendees from 54 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Medellín dbt Meetup
4.7
20 reviews
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

events.raw_text:

Wed, Sep 4, 2024 · 7:00 PM to 9:00 PM COT

Cra. 42 #5 Sur-145

Carrera 42 #5 Sur-145 · Medellín, El Poblado, Medellín, An

Medellín dbt Meetup September
J

Hosted by Juan David R. and dbt L.

dbt L. is a Super Organizer

Medellín dbt Meetup
4.7
20 reviews
Details

Reserve your seat for the next Medellin dbt meetup on the 4th of September 2024 host by Factored! dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🏠Venue Host: We Work Los Balsos, Carrera 42 #5 Sur-145
🍕Catering: Pizza & Beers
🤝Organizer: Factored (https://factored.ai/)
⏰ Time: 6:30pm - 9:00pm

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

📣 Agenda and additional details below:

6:30 pm - 7:00 pm Check in/Registration
7:00 pm - 7:15 pm Welcome Remarks
7:15 pm - 7:35 pm Talk #1 + Q&A
7:35 pm - 7:55 pm Talk #2 + 1 Q&A
7:55 pm - 8:00 pm Closing Remarks and call for speakers for next time.
8:00 pm - 8:45 pm | Reception (45 min)

🗣️Talk #1: dbt turbocharge: Optimización de queries en dbt. En esta charla, nos centraremos en la optimización de queries para los usuarios de dbt, sin limitar nuestro enfoque a un plataforma de datos en específico. Analizaremos las características comunes de la mayoría de SQL engines y proporcionaremos consejos y estrategias prácticas para mejorar el rendimiento de los modelos de dbt.
By Juan Manuel Perafan from Xebia : Trabajo como consultor en Analytics Engineering para Xebia. Además de mi trabajo, soy coautor del libro "Fundamentals of Analytics Engineering", recibí el dbt Community Award y soy el anfitrión del pódcast "SQL Lingua Franca". También soy el fundador/organizador del Analytics Engineering meetup y del dbt meetup en Ámsterdam, y he dado charlas en varias conferencias, incluyendo Coalesce 2023.

🗣️Talk #2: Generación de Datos Sintéticos en dbt: Extrayendo Valor de Datos No Estructurados con LLMs: En esta charla, descubrirás cómo usar LLMs dentro de tu entorno dbt. Exploraremos un enfoque práctico para categorizar datos de texto no estructurados directamente en tu flujo de trabajo de dbt, que abarca tareas como configurar un proyecto dbt para trabajar con la API de OpenAI, crear un modelo en Python sobre dbt, procesar y categorizar datos de texto y transformarlos en conjuntos de datos útiles para análisis y modelado. Ya seas ingeniero de datos, analista o científico de datos, esta charla te proporcionará valiosas ideas sobre cómo integrar la clasificación de texto impulsada por IA en tus pipelines de transformación de datos, mejorando tu capacidad para extraer insights significativos de datos no estructurados.
By Masly Alexandra Velásquez Monsalve from La Haus - Soy Ingeniera de Sistemas, especialista en big data e inteligencia de negocios, y estudiante de antropología. Siempre he trabajado en áreas de tecnologìa y los últimos 5 años en roles de datos. Actualmente trabajo como Ingeniera de Datos en La Haus

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-colombia in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
----------------------------------
dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

When: June 27 @ 4pm EST

Whether you’re looking at flight data, sales figures, consumer transactions, or biometric stats — access to the correct sources + a well-trained user + BI tools + dbt = data that works!!
This month we have Alex Welch, Head of Data at dbt Labs, to answer questions and discuss more about BI tools, analytics trends & the Semantic Layer!

🗣️Talk #1: Ensuring Reliable Data with dbt Macros and Observability: Monitoring Solutions for data quality issues. We'll explore the practical application of dbt Macros in tackling common data quality challenges such as data inconsistency. Attendees will uncover how dbt Macros aid in capturing observability and quality measurements for monitoring purposes, which are crucial for ensuring the reliability of data and stable data pipelines.
By Sebastian Rojas from Factored - Analytics engineer with experience in CPGs and e-commerce industries

🗣️Talk #2: Streamlining Tableau Dashboard Deployment with dbt: Maintaining dashboards gets more complicated as companies grow and business logic is embedded across different places. We will see how dbt can aid in dashboard deployment by consolidating dispersed domain knowledge, in order to increase data reliability, metric consistency and easier dashboard maintenance.
By Sebastian Quintero from Factored - A physicist that found his way in the world of data. With over 5 years of experience, he is now working as an Data Analyst / Analytics Engineer in the fintech industry, providing scalable and optimized data products to drive business decision making.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/

Reserve your seat for the next Medellin dbt meetup on the 28th of Nov 2023 host by Factored! dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🏠Venue Host: We Work Los Balsos, Carrera 42 #5 Sur-145
🍕Catering: Pizza & Beers
🤝Organizer: Factored (https://factored.ai/)
⏰ Time: 6:30pm - 9:00pm

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

📣 Agenda and additional details below:

6:30 pm - 7:00 pm Check in/Registration
7:00 pm - 7:15 pm Welcome Remarks
7:15 pm - 7:35 pm Talk #1 + Q&A
7:35 pm - 7:55 pm Talk #2 + 1 Q&A
7:55 pm - 8:00 pm Closing Remarks and call for speakers for next time.
8:00 pm - 8:45 pm | Reception (45 min)

🗣️Talk #1: Data Engineers vs. Data Analysts: How dbt Breaks Barriers and Redefines Collaboration. - At this talk we will explore the evolution of data engineer and data analyst roles within a contemporary data management environment , with a focus on the pivotal role played by dbt in this transformation. Attendees will discover how dbt is breaking down traditional barriers and reshaping the way engineers and analysts collaborate to extract, transform, and leverage data.
By Miguel Ortiz - A systems engineer passionate about the data world and technology, He works at La Haus as a Data Analyst.

🗣️Talk #2: How Slow Changing Dimensions are Handled on dbt - In this presentation, we'll delve into the world of data modeling, focusing on the power of incremental models and snapshots. Incremental models become your go-to solution when your data loads become too colossal for a full refresh. They allow you to upload only the new or modified data, but handling them comes with its own set of challenges. You'll need to consider the possibility of previous records changing, avoid duplicates, and ensure your logic holds up even when a complete data overhaul is needed.
Additionally, snapshots offer a historical record of your data, freezing specific moments in time – like daily snapshots or captures triggered by specific changes. These snapshots play a crucial role in data auditing. Keep in mind that once a snapshot is taken, you can't turn back the clock because source data continually evolves.
By Steven Salgado - I specialize in crafting user-friendly data models for dashboards and self-service analytics.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-colombia in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

🗣️Talk #1: Let’s discover how our day-to-day “problems” in data teams can become an excellent opportunity to improve our processes with the right tool and add value to organizations.
By Santiago Jaramillo - A data engineer passionate about technology and developments around data.

🗣️Talk #2: As we unveil a game-changing approach to our data architecture of a healthcare company. Witness the migration of an ETL pipeline primarily reliant on Excel and BigQuery scheduled queries to a scalable, robust solution. By leveraging the power of dagster, airbyte, and dbt, we empowered our data pipeline eliminating our bottlenecks and most common problems.
By Pablo Osorio - A data enthusiast who lives for top-notch data quality and rock-solid pipelines built on best code practices.
